title: Butternut soup – with a kick
excerpt: Look, I'm not mad about butternut soup. Especially not when it has that orange-ish taste to it. You usually get it at big functions, because it's cost-effective. At our Fruit&Veg butternuts were on special today. So I decided to make soup. Here's my soup with a kick. …

seo.title: Butternut soup - with a kick - Marinda Kook

html:
Look, I'm not mad about butternut soup. Especially not when it has that orange-ish taste to it. You usually get it at big functions, because it's cost-effective. At our Fruit&Veg butternuts were on special today. So I decided to make soup. Here's my soup with ‘n kick. Absolutely delicious.

  1. 1 large onion or 2 small onions
  2. 30 ml sunflower oil
  3. 10 cups butternut (one very large butternut or 2 small ones)
  4. 2.5 litres water
  5. 60 ml chicken stock powder
  6. 30 ml curry powder
  7. 60 ml chilli powder (or as strong as you like it)
  8. 250 ml cream or 400ml coconut cream
  9. salt and pepper to taste
  10. coriander leaves for serving

NOW:

  • chop your onions finely and fry in a little oil on the stove

  • add your 10 cups of butternut to the onions and fry briefly

  • in another bowl mix the rest of the ingredients except the coriander and the cream. Pour it over your onions and butternut and cook. It must simmer gently.

  • cook for about 20 minutes until the butternut is soft

  • use a food processor or something similar and puree everything – if you don't have one, mash it with your potato masher.

  • now you can choose whether you want to stir in cream or coconut cream
  • taste and season with salt and pepper to taste

Enjoy your soup with a kick with warm bread and a thick layer of butter. For serving you can put the green coriander leaves on top.
